meta.note: Spain (Franco) + Portugal (Estado Novo) + Pakistan sweep, published books. Deduped against existing ES/PT/PK records and the wider DB. Lady Chatterley's Lover, Lolita and Madame Bovary already existed -> Portugal ban-add via existing_book_id (no Portuguese-titled duplicates). In Evil Hour / La mala hora = one García Márquez book (single entry). Held: El tragaluz (Spain, premiere/performance restriction), My Feudal Lord (Pakistan, low confidence). Portugal sourced largely from the official Plano Nacional de Leitura censored-books register; Spain from AGA/academic + Zenda; Pakistan from Dawn/RFE/LRB/PEIRA reporting.

entries.title: Luuanda
entries.description_ban: Angolan short-story collection seized by PIDE; after it won the Portuguese Writers' Society prize while the author was jailed at Tarrafal, the regime banned references to the prize and shut the society down.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Estado Novo seizure; documented in Revista Brasileira de História (SciELO).

entries.title: Engrenagem
entries.description_ban: Posthumous neo-realist novel seized by the censorship board on release for its overt Marxist content.
entries.inclusion_rationale: On the official Plano Nacional de Leitura register of books censored under the Estado Novo.
entries.source_name: Plano Nacional de Leitura — Lista de Livros Censurados

entries.title: Seara de Vento
entries.description_ban: Neo-realist Alentejo novel; the second edition was prohibited and seized from bookshops for depicting oligarchic and GNR oppression of peasants.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Estado Novo ban; documented in WikiAlgarve's 'Livros Proibidos' with censorship docs.

entries.title: Cerromaior
entries.description_ban: Manuel da Fonseca's first neo-realist novel, listed among his prohibited works under the Estado Novo.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Estado Novo ban; documented in WikiAlgarve (medium confidence).

entries.title: O Judeu
entries.description_ban: Play published as a book to escape stage censorship; uses the Inquisition trial of António José da Silva as an allegory of Salazarist/PIDE persecution, prohibited until after the 1974 revolution.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban of the published text; documented via Wikipedia and the Teatro Nacional D. Maria II censorship archive.

entries.title: O Arcanjo Negro
entries.description_ban: Novel drawing on Ribeiro's anti-dictatorship activism; written 1939–40 but blocked by censorship until 1947, and listed among censored titles.
entries.inclusion_rationale: On the official Plano Nacional de Leitura censored-books register (medium confidence).
entries.source_name: Plano Nacional de Leitura — Lista de Livros Censurados

entries.title: Bel-Ami
entries.description_ban: Maupassant's novel of a social-climbing seducer; among French works banned in Portugal under the Estado Novo (1933–1974). Exact ban year not specified in the register.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Documented in the peer-reviewed study 'Le crayon bleu de la censure' (Interlitteraria) (medium confidence).

entries.title: La Condition Humaine
entries.description_ban: Malraux's revolutionary novel set in the 1927 Shanghai uprising; listed among translated works banned in Portugal under the Estado Novo. Exact ban year not specified in the register.
entries.inclusion_rationale: On the official Plano Nacional de Leitura censored-books register (medium confidence).
entries.source_name: Plano Nacional de Leitura — Lista de Livros Censurados

entries.title: Les Mains Sales
entries.description_ban: Sartre's play about a Communist Party assassination; among the Sartre titles listed as banned in Portugal under the Estado Novo. Exact ban year not specified in the register.
entries.inclusion_rationale: On the official Plano Nacional de Leitura censored-books register (medium confidence).
entries.source_name: Plano Nacional de Leitura — Lista de Livros Censurados

entries.title: Lady Chatterley's Lover
entries.description_ban: Listed among translated works banned in Portugal under the Estado Novo. Adds a Portugal ban to the existing record; exact ban year not specified in the register.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Portugal ban; on the official Plano Nacional de Leitura censored-books register.
entries.source_name: Plano Nacional de Leitura — Lista de Livros Censurados

entries.title: Lolita
entries.description_ban: Listed among translated works banned in Portugal under the Estado Novo. Adds a Portugal ban to the existing record; exact ban year not specified in the register.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Portugal ban; on the official Plano Nacional de Leitura censored-books register.
entries.source_name: Plano Nacional de Leitura — Lista de Livros Censurados

entries.title: Madame Bovary
entries.description_ban: Flaubert's adultery novel; among French-authored works banned in Portugal 1933–1974. Adds a Portugal ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Portugal ban; documented in the peer-reviewed study 'Le crayon bleu de la censure' (Interlitteraria).

entries.title: Réquiem por un campesino español
entries.description_ban: Published in exile in Mexico (as 'Mosén Millán') because Francoist censorship blocked it; not published in Spain until late 1974.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Francoist ban; documented via Wikipedia with citation.

entries.title: El rey y la reina
entries.description_ban: Written and published in exile; not published in Spain until 1970 because the author was a Republican exile.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Francoist ban; documented by Zenda (Letras clandestinas).

entries.title: La fiel infantería
entries.description_ban: Won the 1943 National Literature Prize, then seized from bookshops in January 1944 after the Archbishop of Toledo objected to its coarse language; not reissued until 1959.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific seizure; documented in academic commentary (Ínsula Barañaria).

entries.title: Javier Mariño
entries.description_ban: Withdrawn from bookshops roughly twenty days after publication and subjected to numerous cuts for political content.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific withdrawal; documented by Zenda.

entries.title: La familia de Pascual Duarte
entries.description_ban: The 1943 second edition was prohibited on moral/violence grounds; the third edition had to appear in Buenos Aires.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban of the second edition; documented by Zenda.

entries.title: San Camilo, 1936
entries.description_ban: Censors forced cuts; the novel on the outbreak of the Civil War in Madrid circulated only in a mutilated form.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific censorship; documented in a Dialnet academic article.

entries.title: In Evil Hour
entries.description_ban: The 1962 Madrid edition (via INLE), 'La mala hora', was altered by a censor-proofreader; the author disavowed it as adulterated and had unsold copies destroyed.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific censorship of the Spanish edition; documented via Wikipedia and academic sources.

entries.title: Cancionero y romancero de ausencias
entries.description_ban: Prison poems of the condemned Republican poet; suppressed in Spain and first published in Argentina, with his work officially restricted until after Franco.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented via Wikipedia (medium confidence).

entries.title: Español del éxodo y del llanto
entries.description_ban: First major exile collection of the Republican poet; his poetry was prohibited in Spain on political grounds throughout the dictatorship.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented in an academic thesis (medium confidence).

entries.title: La vida sencilla
entries.description_ban: The Spanish ecclesiastical hierarchy deemed the Spanish translation Lutheran/blasphemous and its illustrations mocking of Church dogma; the second edition was prohibited.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Francoist religious ban; documented via Wikipedia (Censura durante el franquismo).

entries.title: Campo de sangre
entries.description_ban: Part of Aub's 'Laberinto mágico' Civil War cycle; among his testimonial works blocked by Francoist censorship (the AGA holds 115 censorship files on Aub).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented in an OpenEdition academic article (medium confidence).

entries.title: After the Prophet
entries.description_ban: A Punjab Assembly committee ordered the book banned and copies confiscated for allegedly blasphemous content about the Prophet and his companions.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific provincial (Punjab) ban; documented by Dawn.

entries.title: The First Muslim
entries.description_ban: Banned alongside Hazleton's other book by the same Punjab Assembly body for purported blasphemy.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific provincial (Punjab) ban; documented by Dawn.

entries.title: A Short History of Islam
entries.description_ban: Long-standard Pakistani student text banned by the Punjab Assembly committee as 'blasphemous', despite drawing on classical Muslim historians.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific provincial (Punjab) ban; documented by Dawn and The Friday Times.

entries.title: I Am Not the Accused, I Am the Complainant
entries.description_ban: Pashtun Tahafuz Movement activist's prison memoir banned amid the state crackdown on the PTM; the author vowed to challenge the ban in court.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific national ban; documented by RFE/RL Gandhara.

entries.title: Can Pakistan Survive? The Death of a State
entries.description_ban: Penguin title banned under Gen. Zia-ul-Haq for arguing the state could disintegrate; circulated in pirated editions.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by Tariq Ali in the London Review of Books (medium confidence).

entries.title: The History and Culture of Pakistan
entries.description_ban: O-level textbook banned in Islamabad by PEIRA notification over an 'inaccurate' map of Pakistan; printing, distribution and teaching prohibited.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific federal-capital ban; documented via PEIRA notification reporting (medium confidence).

entries.title: Sociology (Cambridge O Level Coursebook)
entries.description_ban: Cambridge O-level coursebook banned in Islamabad by the Federal Education Ministry/PEIRA for LGBTQ content; teaching and distribution forbidden.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific federal-capital ban; documented via PEIRA notification reporting (medium confidence).
